construct: Demonstrates proficient use of listening, speaking, reading and writing skills in real-life situations, adapting to audience and purpose to communicate effectively and support life-long learning.

elementsOfConstruct.description: Reads, interprets, summarises and translates continuous and non-continuous texts

subSkills.label: Reads/comprehends texts; interprets vocabulary; summarises; translates preserving meaning/tone

elementsOfConstruct.description: Demonstrates mastery of written language conventions and transformations

subSkills.label: Spells accurately; applies grammar/syntax/punctuation; transforms written structures
Identifies main ideas in spoken texts; analyses purpose/tone; follows instructions; interprets spoken source→target
subSkills.note: Primarily SBA; Final Exam focuses on EoC1-2.

elementsOfConstruct.description: Produces ideas, thoughts and opinions through spoken language

subSkills.label: Organises ideas; speaks clearly/fluently; appropriate vocabulary/tone; accurate pronunciation/grammar
subSkills.note: Primarily SBA; Final Exam focuses on EoC1-2.

components.structureNotes: SBA Form 2: 5 tasks/90. All 4 EoC. Classwork, homework, oral presentations, projects. Plus shared project 20 marks across Form 2-3.
Final Exam EoC1+2 only: Section A Reading (2 items); Section B Structure+Translation; Section C Writing. SBA also includes Form2-3 project (20 marks). Weighting SBA:FE 40:60. Duration 2h.

rules: SBA marked at school (40% with project); Final Exam centrally marked (60%).
Reading passages 250-300 words; semi-structured tasks typically 15 marks each.
Composition: guided and unguided (10 marks each part).

exemplars.scenarioTheme: French continuous/non-continuous reading scenarios with semi-structured comprehension tasks
